summary: A Go-based monolithic tool leveraging MCP for orchestration and providing Goss migration capabilities via an internal runner.
concerns: High volume of agent-generated extra tests (*_extra_test.go) suggests potential test fragility or over-testing
Scattered baseline and reporter implementations across agent directories indicate possible merge conflicts or duplication
Clean git status with recent metadata commits implies heavy automated CI/agent-driven development workflow
suggestions: Consolidate duplicated internal packages (baseline, reporter) from agent directories into the main internal/ tree
Evaluate if extra test files provide unique value or just inflate test runtime
Focus on core runner.go and MCP handlers for actual feature development rather than test expansion
